agent.system_template:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"{{ task }}"

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of {{ step_budget }} movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
agent.instance_template: Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.

tools.name: observe
tools.description:
Look through the robot's forward-facing camera.

Returns the current egocentric RGB view plus a clearance readout: distance in meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view (10.0 = open, 10 m or more). If the goal object is centered ahead, clearance "center" is your true distance to it. Pure read — does not advance the simulator or consume step budget.

tools.name: look_around
tools.description:
Scan the surroundings in ONE call: rotates a full 360 degrees and
    returns four labeled views — ahead (0°), right (+90°), behind (+180°),
    left (+270°) — then restores the original heading exactly.

    Costs 24 low-level turn steps from the step budget but only one tool
    call. Use this at junctions and decision points instead of turning 15
    degrees at a time; your position and final heading are unchanged.

tools.name: step
tools.description:
Execute a sequence of movement actions, in order.

Actions: 0 = STOP (permanently ENDS the episode — issue it only when you believe the robot is within 3 meters of the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.

Executes sequentially and halts early if the episode ends. Returns how many actions ran, total steps taken, remaining budget, and whether the episode is over. The camera view changes after stepping — call observe() to see the result. Note: when plenty of budget remains, your FIRST STOP request is withheld pending a placement check — call step([0]) again to confirm and execute it.

messages.content:
You are controlling a robot in a real indoor environment (a photorealistic 3D scan of a building). You interact only through these tools:

- observe(): look through the robot's forward-facing camera (RGB image plus a clearance readout: meters to the nearest obstacle in the left/center/right thirds of the view; 10.0 = open).
- step(actions): execute movement actions in order. 0 = STOP (permanently ends the episode — declares you have reached the goal), 1 = move forward 0.25 m, 2 = turn left 15 degrees, 3 = turn right 15 degrees.
- look_around(): one call returning four labeled views (ahead / right / behind / left); rotates 360 degrees and restores your heading (costs 24 turn steps).

Your task is to follow this navigation instruction to its endpoint:

"Walk up stairs. At top of stairs turn right. Walk straight to bedroom. Turn left and walk to bed lamp.Turn left and enter closet. Stop at rug. "

Rules:
- Alternate observing and stepping: look, decide where the instruction wants you to go next, move, look again.
- You have a budget of 500 movement actions; each step() result reports roughly how many remain.
- You succeed only if you issue action 0 (STOP) while within 3 meters of the instruction's endpoint. STOP is permanent — issue it only when you believe you are at the goal.
- Turning in place (e.g. step([2,2,2,2,2,2])) is a cheap way to look around when unsure.
- Work autonomously until you stop; nobody can answer questions.
Begin navigating. Call observe() first to see where you are.
I'll start by observing my current surroundings.

message.content: I'll start by observing my current surroundings.

messages.content: I see what looks like an indoor space with a mirror and doors. I need to find stairs. Let me look around to get a full view.

message.content: I see what looks like an indoor space with a mirror and doors. I need to find stairs. Let me look around to get a full view.

messages.content: I can see stairs in both the "behind" and "left" views. Let me turn left to face the staircase and go up.

message.content: I can see stairs in both the "behind" and "left" views. Let me turn left to face the staircase and go up.

messages.content: I can see the stairs going up now. Let me move forward to start climbing.

message.content: I can see the stairs going up now. Let me move forward to start climbing.

messages.content: Now I can see the stairs continue upward with a curve. Let me continue climbing.

message.content: Now I can see the stairs continue upward with a curve. Let me continue climbing.

messages.content: I appear to be at the top of the stairs now. According to the instructions, I need to turn right at the top. Let me look around first.

message.content: I appear to be at the top of the stairs now. According to the instructions, I need to turn right at the top. Let me look around first.

messages.content: I'll turn right as instructed at the top of the stairs.

message.content: I'll turn right as instructed at the top of the stairs.

messages.content: This looks like a good path forward. Let me continue walking straight.

message.content: This looks like a good path forward. Let me continue walking straight.

messages.content: I can see a bedroom ahead with a bed visible. This matches the instruction to "walk straight to bedroom." Let me continue forward.

message.content: I can see a bedroom ahead with a bed visible. This matches the instruction to "walk straight to bedroom." Let me continue forward.

messages.content: I'm getting closer to the bedroom entrance. Let me continue forward into the bedroom.

message.content: I'm getting closer to the bedroom entrance. Let me continue forward into the bedroom.

messages.content: I'm now in the bedroom with the bed visible on the left. I can see a lamp near the bed. Let me turn left as instructed.

message.content: I'm now in the bedroom with the bed visible on the left. I can see a lamp near the bed. Let me turn left as instructed.

messages.content: I can see the bedroom with the bed clearly in the "right" view. Let me turn right to face the bed and lamp.

message.content: I can see the bedroom with the bed clearly in the "right" view. Let me turn right to face the bed and lamp.

messages.content: Now I can see the bed with a lamp on the left side. Let me walk toward it.

message.content: Now I can see the bed with a lamp on the left side. Let me walk toward it.

messages.content: Now I can clearly see the bed with lamps on both nightstands. Let me approach closer to the lamp.

message.content: Now I can clearly see the bed with lamps on both nightstands. Let me approach closer to the lamp.

messages.content: I've reached the bed with lamps. Now I need to turn left again to head toward the closet.

message.content: I've reached the bed with lamps. Now I need to turn left again to head toward the closet.

messages.content: I can see a doorway ahead that might be the closet, and a lamp on the dresser. Let me look around for more context.

message.content: I can see a doorway ahead that might be the closet, and a lamp on the dresser. Let me look around for more context.

messages.content: This looks like a closet with shelving and a rug visible! This matches the instruction "enter closet. Stop at rug." Let me move closer to investigate.

message.content: This looks like a closet with shelving and a rug visible! This matches the instruction "enter closet. Stop at rug." Let me move closer to investigate.

messages.content: I can see the closet area on the left. Let me enter it.

message.content: I can see the closet area on the left. Let me enter it.

messages.content: This closet doesn't have a rug. Let me check other areas. Let me look around from here.

message.content: This closet doesn't have a rug. Let me check other areas. Let me look around from here.

messages.content: Let me go back to check the area near the entrance where I recall seeing a rug.

message.content: Let me go back to check the area near the entrance where I recall seeing a rug.

messages.content: Let me go back toward where I first saw a rug near the closet entrance.

message.content: Let me go back toward where I first saw a rug near the closet entrance.
